intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good — I see three 10x samples in the data folder, with the first one being **GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1** as you specified. Now let me load the Seurat recipe to see the standard workflow:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Now I have the recipe. Let me present a plan for the workflow, noting that the recipe highlights three key decision points (QC thresholds, PCs, clustering resolution):

input.title: Seurat v5 workflow for GSM5746268_SLAB-C1-C1
input.summary: Load 10x matrix triplet, QC filter, normalize, find variable features, scale, PCA, neighbors, cluster with Leiden resolution, UMAP, and identify top markers per cluster.
input.rationale: Standard scRNA-seq processing pipeline to identify cell types and states.
input.assumptions:
- Species: mouse (MGI genes, hence 'MT-' prefix for mitochondrial genes)
- Use default QC thresholds (nFeature_RNA ≥ 200, percent.mt ≤ 10%) unless you prefer stricter/looser bounds
- Read off ElbowPlot to select PCs (typically 20–30 for well-resolved data)
- Use resolution = 0.4–0.8 for initial clustering (tunable after seeing results)

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet into Seurat object
steps.description: Read .matrix.mtx, .features.tsv, .barcodes.tsv and CreateSeuratObject with min.cells=3, min.features=200

steps.title: QC: Calculate per-cell metrics and filter
steps.description: PercentageFeatureSet for MT-* genes, visualize nFeature, nCount, percent.mt; subset on thresholds (default: nFeature ≥ 200, percent.mt ≤ 10%)
expected_outputs: QC scatter plot showing pre/post filter; filtered object

steps.title: Normalize, find variable features, scale
expected_outputs: Seurat object with normalized counts and variable features

steps.title: PCA and determine dimensionality
steps.description: RunPCA → ElbowPlot to visualize explained variance and choose n.pcs

steps.title: Compute neighbors, cluster, and UMAP
expected_outputs: Seurat object with clusters and UMAP; UMAP colored by cluster

steps.title: Find and display top markers per cluster
steps.description: JoinLayers → FindAllMarkers → extract top N genes per cluster and display as heatmap/table
expected_outputs: Top markers table; heatmap of top markers across clusters
